title: Structured artifacts[] on StepResult — no first-class pointer to files a step produced
body: StepResult has outputPath (single file path where the output was persisted). There is no structured list of artifacts a step produced, no kind classification, no way for a step to say 'I produced these 3 files, these 2 are source code, this 1 is a report'. step-block.ts writes to .project/ blocks but those writes are not returned as artifacts on the StepResult. context-packet's Packet has artifacts: Array<{path: string, kind: string}> — structured file pointers separate from the packet body. Extension: StepResult should include artifacts: Array<{path: string, kind: string, summary?: string}>. Enables downstream steps to reference artifacts by path and kind without loading them into the prompt; enables block write-back monitors (issue-030) to observe what a step produced.
